title: HTTP signature verification: draft-cavage-11, key caching, re-fetch on failure
description:
Verify HTTP signatures on all inbound ActivityPub POST requests before processing. Use activitypub-federation crate's verification. Key lookup: extract keyId from Signature header, fetch actor document (or use cached public_key_pem from remote_accounts), verify signature. On verification failure: attempt one re-fetch of actor document in case key was rotated, then try again before returning 401.

**Agent workflow**: Spawn a research subagent to study the activitypub-federation crate's signature verification API in detail. Spawn a testing subagent to capture a real signed request from mastodon.social (using a test webhook receiver) and write verification tests against it.

**Test discipline**:
- Capture a real signed POST from mastodon.social to use as test fixture
- Test valid signature: verification passes
- Test tampered body (change one byte): verification fails with clear error
- Test expired Date header (>30s old): verification fails
- Test missing Signature header: 401 immediately
- Test keyId pointing to unknown actor: attempt fetch, if fetch fails return 401
Fixtures must be real captured requests — never synthesized by the implementation under test. Use real in-memory SQLite for DB tests.

**Defensive programming**:
- Consume and buffer the request body before verification — can only read once
- Hard timeout on key fetch (5s) — don't let a slow remote server stall our inbox
- Never expose signature verification failure details to the caller — 401 with generic message
- Log verification failures at WARN with the sending domain for monitoring
- No .unwrap() outside test code; all errors are Result<T, AppError>
acceptance_criteria: Real mastodon.social signed request passes verification; tampered body fails; expired Date fails; missing Signature returns 401; key fetch timeout returns 401 within 5s
